title: Unit 1 — Which principle is most violated in current AI systems?

children.body: This week we covered the four foundational principles of AI ethics: autonomy, beneficence, non-maleficence, and justice. The lecture ended with an open question: which of these do you think is most frequently violated in current AI systems, and why? Please share your reasoning — not just your answer.

I think justice is the most violated principle. AI systems tend to work better for majority groups because that is who the training data represents. Facial recognition is a clear example — error rates are much higher for darker skin tones. I agree with the lecture that this is a structural problem, not a technical glitch.
I agree with student1. Justice is the obvious answer here. The lecture made this point very clearly. Training data reflects existing inequalities, so the system inherits them.
I also agree. Justice seems like the most violated one. The examples from the lecture were very convincing.
Same here. Justice. The facial recognition example really stood out to me as well.
I agree with everyone above. Justice is clearly the most violated principle in practice.
